steps.title: Use the head-width prediction
steps.content: The hypothesis predicts that in low visibility, wider-headed fish will be less likely than narrower-headed fish to detect obstructions. That means wider-headed fish should bump into more obstructions.

steps.title: Require a direct comparison
steps.content: A gives only wide-headed fish without a comparison, B describes variation within a narrow-headed species, and D shows equal performance. The matching finding compares wider-headed and narrower-headed fish and shows the wider-headed fish bumping into more obstructions. The answer is C.
